metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate both verifier prompt targets in Scope: add `plan-review-verifier-agentic` alongside `plan-review-verifier` as explicit files to update with the 7 new PlanSeverityAttrs axes.
findings.finding: The Scope section names 'the plan-review verify prompt' (singular) as an edit target for emitting the 7 new PlanSeverityAttrs axes, but the gate YAML (plan-review.yaml) reveals TWO distinct verifier prompt IDs: `plan-review-verifier` (single-turn) and `plan-review-verifier-agentic`. Both prompts must be updated to emit the 7 new axes or the agentic code-grounded path will never populate the new fields, leaving impact_plan operating on all-default (zero-contributing) axes for code-grounded findings.
findings.location: Scope section: 'the plan-review verify prompt (emit the 7 axes)'; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml lines 144 and 234
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'the plan-review verify prompt (emit the 7 axes)' to 'the plan-review verifier prompts (`plan-review-verifier` and `plan-review-verifier-agentic`) — emit the 7 axes in both'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the ordinal vocabulary (e.g., none|low|medium|high) and [0,1] mapping for each of the 7 new `PlanSeverityAttrs` axes, and add at least a brief note on how verifier reliability for these axes was validated or will be validated.
findings.finding: The 7 new SEVERITY axes in `PlanSeverityAttrs` (AC-unverifiable, DoD-uncertifiable, undecomposed, divergent-implementation, internal-conflict, vague-directive, irreversible-without-rationale) are described as 'each mapped to [0,1] by the verify pass', but their ordinal vocabulary, concrete [0,1] mapping, and whether the LLM verifier can reliably emit them are unverified capability assertions.
findings.location: ## What — SEVERITY axes list; ## Implementation mechanics — PlanSeverityAttrs
findings.suggested_fix: For each new axis, add a `Field(default='none', description='none|low|medium|high — <concise definition>')` in `PlanSeverityAttrs`, mirroring the existing `SeverityAttrs` pattern. Reference the mapping table (e.g., none→0.0, low→0.33, medium→0.67, high→1.0) in the AC or Implementation mechanics section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the full value vocabulary (enum set + ordinal-to-[0,1] mapping) for each of the 7 new `PlanSeverityAttrs` axes — specify whether they share the `none|low|medium|high` scale of existing `SeverityAttrs` fields or use a distinct vocabulary (e.g., boolean `present|absent`).
findings.finding: The 7 new `PlanSeverityAttrs` fields (AC-unverifiable, DoD-uncertifiable, undecomposed, divergent-implementation, internal-conflict, vague-directive, irreversible-without-rationale) are listed by name but their value vocabulary (the enum set the verifier LLM must emit) is never defined. The plan says each defaults to `'none'/absent` but is silent on the full allowed set — are these boolean, ordinal (`none|low|medium|high`), or something else? The existing `SeverityAttrs` fields each carry an explicit `description="none|low|medium|high"` constraint. Without a defined vocabulary the verifier prompt cannot emit conformant values and `impact_plan`'s MAX aggregation has no ordinal mapping to apply.
findings.location: ## What — 'SEVERITY axes'; ## Implementation mechanics — 'PlanSeverityAttrs adding the 7 axis fields above, EACH defaulting to none/absent'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Implementation mechanics section: 'Each of the 7 new axes uses the same `none|low|medium|high` scale as the existing `SeverityAttrs` fields, mapped by the existing `_SEV01` table; `none` = 0.0, `low` = 0.33, `medium` = 0.67, `high` = 1.0. The verifier prompt must include the enum description string `none|low|medium|high` in each field's `Field(description=...)` — matching the existing pattern.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the Scope section to name both verifier prompts (`plan-review-verifier` and `plan-review-verifier-agentic`) as edit targets for the 7 new axis emission instructions.
findings.finding: The plan names 'the plan-review verify prompt' (singular) as a Scope edit target for emitting the 7 new `PlanSeverityAttrs` axes, but the live gate YAML (`plan-review.yaml`) uses TWO distinct verifier prompts: `plan-review-verifier` (single-turn, else-arm) and `plan-review-verifier-agentic` (agentic, then-arm), branched on `code_grounded`. Both prompts must emit the new axes for the schema contract to hold, but only one edit target is named. This is a pink-elephant omission: the second prompt is silently left without the new axis instructions.
findings.location: ## Scope — 'the plan-review verify prompt (emit the 7 axes)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'the plan-review verify prompt (emit the 7 axes)' to 'both plan-review verifier prompts (`plan-review-verifier` and `plan-review-verifier-agentic`) (emit the 7 axes in each)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC4's vague 'offline replay' clause with: (a) a named fixture (e.g. `tests/fixtures/plan_review_replay.jsonl`) containing ≥1 historical finding whose old mean-impact score was in [0.60, 0.69], (b) a concrete proving command (e.g. `pytest tests/test_impact_plan.py::test_replay_clears_threshold -v`), and (c) a stability assertion (e.g. 'verdicts on fixture pairs A/B match to within ±0 bits on re-run').
findings.finding: AC4 ('Offline replay') has no named fixture, no concrete proving command, and uses undefined terms ('previously-stranded 0.60-0.69 band findings', 'same-material replicate pairs'), making it unevaluable in-session and leaving the production cutover to `impact_plan` unverified end-to-end.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'Offline replay: previously-stranded 0.60-0.69 band findings clear 0.70; verdicts stay stable on same-material replicate pairs.'
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace AC4 with:
```
- [ ] Offline replay: given `tests/fixtures/plan_review_replay.jsonl` (≥1 finding whose legacy `mean(severity_attrs)` ∈ [0.60, 0.69]), running `pytest tests/test_impact_plan.py::test_replay_clears_threshold` passes, asserting `impact_plan(finding) >= 0.70` for every fixture entry; a second run on the same fixture produces byte-identical verdicts (stability check).
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the source and schema of the `silent-vs-self-revealing` axis: is it a new field in `PlanSeverityAttrs` emitted by the LLM verifier, a deterministic heuristic, or something else? Define its vocabulary and how it maps to the x1.0/x0.8 multipliers.
findings.finding: The 'DETECTION AMPLIFIER' parameter `silent-vs-self-revealing` is introduced as a new scored axis but has no defined source (which model/verifier emits it), no ordinal vocabulary, and no mapping rule — making it an unverified capability assertion about a construct that has no established precedent in this codebase or any cited reference.
findings.location: ## What — DETECTION AMPLIFIER; AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the sourcing rule for the `silent-vs-self-revealing` classification: state whether it is (a) a new field in `PlanSeverityAttrs` the verifier LLM emits (and if so, add it to the schema with its vocabulary), (b) a static property keyed to each criterion id in the criteria registry, or (c) computed from existing fields — and co-locate the rule with the schema definition.
findings.finding: The `silent-vs-self-revealing` classification that drives the DETECTION AMPLIFIER multiplier (x1.0 vs x0.8) is referenced as a known property of each finding, but the plan never specifies where this classification comes from — it is not a field in the new `PlanSeverityAttrs`, not in the existing `SeverityAttrs`, not in the existing `Binary` sub-answer vocabulary, and has no match anywhere in the codebase. An implementer cannot determine whether this is: (a) a new field the verifier emits, (b) a static property of each rubric criterion, (c) a heuristic computed from existing fields, or (d) something else entirely.
findings.location: ## What — 'DETECTION AMPLIFIER: silent-vs-self-revealing (silent build-the-wrong-thing -> x1.0, self-revealing wall -> x0.8)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit unit tests for `impact_plan`: (a) hard-override fires to ≥0.85 when any of {AC-unverifiable, DoD-uncertifiable, undecomposed, divergent-implementation} is present regardless of other axis values; (b) DoD-uncertifiable forces detection multiplier to x1.0; (c) a missing/absent axis contributes 0 and does not inflate impact; (d) a finding with all axes at 'none'/absent produces impact < 0.70 (no false inflation).
findings.finding: The plan introduces new `impact_plan` logic (MAX aggregation, hard-override at ≥0.85 for 4 axis types, detection amplifier with two multipliers, and abstaining-default for missing axes), but specifies NO unit tests for these behaviors. The only test named in AC1 is byte-stability on the old code-review path. AC4's offline replay on previously-stranded findings does not guarantee coverage of the hard-override path, the DoD-uncertifiable x1.0 multiplier forcing, or the zero-contribution abstaining-default (back-compat) path — the replay can go green while these branches are broken.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC1, AC4; 'What' section (impact_plan formula)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Unit tests for `impact_plan` cover: (i) hard-override fires to max(impact_sev, 0.85) for each of the 4 override-triggering axes in isolation; (ii) DoD-uncertifiable sets detection_mult=1.0 regardless of silent/self-revealing value; (iii) all 7 axes absent/defaulted → impact < 0.70; (iv) max-axis selection is taken from the highest contributing axis, not the mean.' These tests must be enumerated in an AC or in the implementation section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in 'Implementation mechanics' exactly how the G5 signal populates `PlanSeverityAttrs.undecomposed`: name the G5 output field, state that the orchestrator writes it into the attrs dict before (or instead of) the verifier emitting it, and confirm whether the verify prompt should emit this axis or read it as a pre-computed input.
findings.finding: The 'undecomposed' axis in PlanSeverityAttrs is described as coming 'from the deterministic G5 signal child' but the plan does not specify the wiring: whether the orchestrator pre-populates this field from G5's output before invoking the verifier, or whether the LLM verifier itself detects and emits it. Without this, the implementer cannot determine whether the axis is deterministic (as claimed) or LLM-evaluated, and the hard-override for 'undecomposed' may never fire if the wiring is wrong.
findings.location: 'What' section (SEVERITY axes list); 'Implementation mechanics' (PlanSeverityAttrs); 'Scope' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the wiring protocol for the `undecomposed` axis: state exactly how the deterministic G5 signal result is injected into `PlanSeverityAttrs.undecomposed` (e.g., post-LLM dict mutation before `reshape_verifications`, a pre-populated context field the verifier echoes, or a separate post-processing step) and identify the precise code location where this happens.
findings.finding: The `undecomposed` axis in `PlanSeverityAttrs` is described as coming from 'the deterministic G5 signal child', but the wiring between that pre-LLM deterministic signal and the Pass-2 `PlanSeverityAttrs.undecomposed` field is never specified. The verification model is populated by the verifier LLM at Pass-2; the G5 deterministic check fires at precheck/Pass-1 time. The plan does not state how a deterministic precheck result gets injected into the per-finding verifier output schema at Pass-2 — whether the verifier LLM is instructed to echo it, whether it is post-processed into the verification dict after the LLM call, or handled some other way.
findings.location: ## What — 'undecomposed (from the deterministic G5 signal child)'; ## Implementation mechanics — 'PlanSeverityAttrs adding the 7 axis fields above'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the 'deterministic G5 signal child' this plan depends on either already exists in the codebase (confirm the source file and symbol) or is a gated prerequisite; if it does not yet exist, either remove `undecomposed` from the initial `PlanSeverityAttrs` (adding it when the child ships) or explicitly sequence this plan after that child.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'undecomposed (from the deterministic G5 signal child)' as one of the 7 severity axes sourced from an already-existing (or separately-planned) deterministic G5 signal. However, G5 in the codebase is exclusively an LLM-tier criterion (registry.py line 99; plan-review.yaml line 82 routes it as an LLM prompt). No deterministic G5 signal exists anywhere in det_floor.py or any source file — searches for 'G5 signal', 'deterministic G5', and 'G5 det_floor' return zero hits. The plan additionally scopes itself as 'Depends on the deterministic G5 signal child', treating this child as a prerequisite. If that child does not yet exist, the `undecomposed` axis in `PlanSeverityAttrs` has no data source and cannot be wired, making AC3 (the 7-axis model) partially undeliverable without first resolving the child dependency.
findings.location: ## What — SEVERITY axes list; ## Scope — 'Depends on the deterministic G5 signal child'; AC3
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is that P4's oversize heuristic (`det_floor.p4_oversize`) supplies the `undecomposed` signal, name that concretely in the Scope section and confirm the wiring path. If a new deterministic check must be built first, add it as a blocking dependency with a ticket reference.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between the detection multiplier formula (0.85 × 0.8 = 0.68) and AC#4's guarantee that 0.60-0.69 findings clear 0.70 — either floor `impact` at 0.70 when the hard override fires, or restrict the detection multiplier from applying to hard-override cases, or revise AC#4 to reflect the actual attainable range.
findings.finding: The 'What' formula allows the hard override (impact_sev=0.85) to be reduced below 0.70 by the detection multiplier (0.85 × 0.8 = 0.68), directly contradicting the 'Why' motivation ('a critical finding averaged with low axes lands ~0.60-0.69') and AC#4 ('previously-stranded 0.60-0.69 band findings clear 0.70'). These cross-section claims are irreconcilable as written.
findings.location: 'What' section (formula) vs 'Why' section vs AC#4
findings.suggested_fix: Add a post-compose floor: `if hard_override_fired: impact = max(impact, 0.70)`, and update AC#4 to state 'findings that trigger the hard override clear 0.70 regardless of detection multiplier'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the hard-override + detection-amplifier interaction so that a hard-override finding is guaranteed to clear 0.70: either add a post-compose floor (`impact = max(impact, 0.70)` when the override fired), restrict the x0.8 multiplier from applying when the override is active, or extend the 'DoD-uncertifiable forces x1.0' rule to cover all four hard-override axes — and update AC4 to reflect the actual guaranteed minimum.
findings.finding: The hard-override + detection-amplifier compose produces 0.85 × 0.8 = 0.68 for self-revealing findings, which falls BELOW the 0.70 bar the plan's 'Why' section and AC4 assert will be cleared. An implementer faithfully implementing the formula will fail AC4 for any finding that triggers the hard override but is classified as self-revealing.
findings.location: 'What' section (HARD OVERRIDE + DETECTION AMPLIFIER); AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'POST-COMPOSE FLOOR: if any hard-override axis fired, `impact = max(impact, 0.70)` (the override guarantees threshold clearance regardless of detection multiplier).' Update AC4 to: 'findings that trigger the hard override clear 0.70; other previously-stranded findings clear 0.70 via the MAX aggregation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either describe the replay infrastructure (fixture format, how replicate pairs are captured/stored, and what 'stable' means quantitatively) in the plan body, or split AC#4 into two separate ACs — one for the threshold-clearance property (mechanically testable) and one for replay stability (requires its own implementation story).
findings.finding: AC#4 requires 'verdicts stay stable on same-material replicate pairs' — an empirical replay stability criterion — but the plan body describes no replay infrastructure, test harness, or data fixture to exercise this. It is an orphan criterion with no described implementation path.
findings.location: AC#4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that explicitly verifies the plan-review verifier prompt emits all 7 axis fields (e.g., a prompt-content assertion or a golden-fixture test where a known finding produces non-abstaining axis values).
findings.finding: The 'Scope' section names the 'plan-review verify prompt (emit the 7 axes)' as a deliverable requiring modification, but no AC covers verification of this prompt change — AC#3 covers only the schema (`plan_review_verification_model()` + `PlanSeverityAttrs`), leaving the prompt itself an uncovered deliverable.
findings.location: Scope section ('the plan-review verify prompt (emit the 7 axes)') vs AC#3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the G5 deterministic signal value is wired into `PlanSeverityAttrs.undecomposed` (e.g., pre-populated field before the verifier runs vs. verifier-detected), and add an observable proof in AC3 or a new AC that the axis fires correctly when G5 signals a finding as undecomposed.
findings.finding: The 'undecomposed' axis in PlanSeverityAttrs is sourced from 'the deterministic G5 signal child' but the plan does not specify how that signal is wired into the verification model or prompt at implementation time — making AC3 ('older/absent verifiers do not inflate impact') partially unverifiable as a proof of end-state correctness.
findings.location: ## What (undecomposed axis); ## Implementation mechanics (PlanSeverityAttrs); AC3
findings.suggested_fix: In the Implementation mechanics section, add: 'The orchestrator populates `PlanSeverityAttrs.undecomposed` from the G5 signal output before invoking `plan_review_verification_model()`; the verify prompt does NOT re-detect decomposition — it reads the pre-populated value.' Then add to AC3: 'A unit test asserts that when G5 sets undecomposed=high, impact_plan ≥ 0.85 (hard-override fires).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md / design-doc section documenting the rationale for the MAX+hard-override+amplifier pattern, the chosen axes, and the `impact_fn` dispatch mechanism, so future gate authors can follow or consciously deviate from it.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — per-gate impact aggregation dispatch via `impact_fn` injection — but mentions no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry to capture the rationale for why MAX+override+amplifier replaces mean and why the specific axes and values were chosen.
findings.location: ## Implementation mechanics — PER-GATE DISPATCH; ## Scope

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default detection multiplier when `silent-vs-self-revealing` is absent or holds an unrecognized value (e.g. 'absent/unknown defaults to x1.0, treating the case as worst-case silent').
findings.finding: The detection multiplier value for the `silent-vs-self-revealing` axis when the field is absent, null, or holds a value other than 'silent' or 'self-revealing' is not stated, leaving the executor to guess the default.
findings.location: What section — 'DETECTION AMPLIFIER: `silent-vs-self-revealing` (silent build-the-wrong-thing -> x1.0, self-revealing wall -> x0.8)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the DETECTION AMPLIFIER bullet: 'absent or unrecognized values default to x1.0 (conservative/worst-case silent assumption).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the `impact_fn` callable injection into `pass3_decide`/`pass3_over_findings` is justified now (≥2 current in-scope consumers) or whether `impact_plan` should instead be applied by the plan-review wrapper (`orchestrator.pass3_over_findings`) before delegating to the kernel, keeping the kernel's shared path free of gate-specific dispatch.
findings.finding: The `impact_fn: Callable[[dict], float] | None = None` injection into `pass3_decide()` and `pass3_over_findings` is premature abstraction: as of this plan's scope, only ONE consumer (plan-review) will pass a non-default `impact_fn`. The plan explicitly places code-review out of scope ('No code-review changes'), and AC1 only requires plan-review to thread `impact_plan`. The callable injection serves a stated-hypothetical future gate, not a current done-definition, violating Rule-of-Three (1 live consumer, not ≥3) and YAGNI.
findings.location: Implementation mechanics / Scope / AC1 — `pass3_decide()` `impact_fn` param
findings.suggested_fix: Rather than adding `impact_fn` to the shared kernel's `pass3_decide`/`pass3_over_findings`, apply `impact_plan` in plan-review's own `orchestrator.pass3_over_findings` wrapper: post-process the `impact` field on each decided finding (or pre-process the attrs before calling the kernel). This keeps the kernel's arithmetic path clean and single-purpose until a second gate concretely needs its own impact function.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extract the 0.85 hard-override threshold and detection multiplier values (x1.0 / x0.8) into named constants or a config struct so they can be tuned without code changes.
findings.finding: The 0.85 hard-override value and the detection multipliers (x1.0 silent, x0.8 self-revealing) are hardcoded policy constants with no mention of making them configurable; these thresholds are likely to be tuned as real replay data accumulates.
findings.location: ## What — HARD OVERRIDE and DETECTION AMPLIFIER; ## Acceptance Criteria AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Define a `PlanReviewImpactConfig(hard_override_floor: float = 0.85, silent_mult: float = 1.0, self_revealing_mult: float = 0.8)` dataclass; pass it into `impact_plan`; thread it from the orchestrator so callers can override without touching the formula code.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief prior-art note to the plan justifying why MAX+override+amplifier is chosen over simpler alternatives (e.g., weighted mean, CVSS-style base-score) that also address mean-dilution.
findings.finding: The plan proposes a custom three-stage impact scoring architecture (MAX aggregation → hard-override → detection amplifier) without citing or dismissing any established prior art — e.g., CVSS base-score max-composition, weighted-mean alternatives, or existing LLM-review scoring frameworks — that address the same mean-dilution problem.
findings.location: ## What — severity-first MAX + hard override + detection amplifier design

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
